_aboutme: ABOUTME: Curated stat-roll variance rule (no variance keys exist in items or records/game/,
ABOUTME: so the engine's jitter is reproduced here; see docs/item-schema.md).
Rule: display_low = ceil(base * (1 - jitter/100)), display_high = floor(base * (1 + jitter/100))
- inward rounding, so the printed range is what the game can actually roll. Item stats use
jitter_percent below; affix stats use their own record's lootRandomizerJitter (0 = fixed).
True Min/Max damage pairs, weapon damage lines, and the exemptions below never roll.

calibration.value: 20 with inward rounding, locked 2026-07-04 against the screenshot corpus
evidence: Sacrificial Knife +8% cold modifier -> 7/9 (8*0.8=6.4 ceil 7; 8*1.2=9.6 floor 9). This resolves the 12.5-vs-20 question in favor of the community's 20: 12.5 only looked right under exact-bound rounding.
Meat Shield: physique 46 -> 37/55; +% health 21 -> 17/25; health regen 40 -> 32/48; constitution 50 -> 40/60; vitality res 35 -> 28/42 (all exact)
The Final Stop: health 360 -> 288/432; stun res 30 -> 24/36 (exact)
Bramblevine: acid retaliation 688 -> 551/825; physical res 12 -> 10/14; elemental res 35 -> 28/42; +% all retaliation 40 -> 32/48 (exact)
calibration.known_gap: Offensive damage-bonus lines on items carrying attributeScalePercent show an extra level-linked upscale on grimtools that plain jitter does not reproduce: Meat Shield offensiveLife 22 -> card 23/33 (implies ~x1.27 at level 50); Bramblevine offensive modifiers 60 -> card 62/93 (implies ~x1.2917 at level 58). v1 ships plain jitter ranges for these lines; the scaling mechanism is a BACKLOG follow-up.
